rationale.R1.1: The artifact advances an explicit central thesis (rationale diversity at decode time is the principal lever) that is materially contradicted by the paper's own ablation conclusion; reconstructing which claim is authoritative is central to any useful reading.
rationale.R1.2: Paragraph 1 asserts inference-time sampling is the principal lever; paragraph 4 asserts distillation—not decode-time aggregation—is the principal driver and that the former 'leaves substantial accuracy on the table.' These are directly inconsistent and load-bearing to evaluate.
rationale.R1.3: The reported numbers (61.8% distilled > 60.4% inference-only; 48.1% vs 44.7% on CLEVRER; 70% shrinkage without distillation) actively undermine the stated thesis, making evidence-claim alignment a central concern.
rationale.R1.6: 'Take a look at this paragraph' is naturally read as a request for writing or presentation feedback; prose quality is therefore plausibly load-bearing.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I focus on logical consistency and argument validity—specifically whether the central thesis in paragraph 1 coheres with the ablation conclusions in paragraph 4—or on writing quality and clarity of exposition?
